mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent unemployment above 9.5% indicates slack in the labor market, prompting central bank intervention and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE > 9.5% are negative or flat (return < 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and payroll employment signal weak supply-side dynamics, creating tailwinds for equity markets through fiscal stimulus expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show negative correlation with combined INDPRO/PAYEMS declines (Rank IC < -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits and consumer spending indicate expanding demand, driving S&P 500 positive returns during post-crisis recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with HOUST/RSAFS growth are negative (return < -1%).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals deteriorating supply conditions, prompting monetary easing that boosts equity valuations over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO decline periods are negative (return < -0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 215) signals accommodative monetary policy, supporting S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPIAUCSL < 215 periods are negative (return < -1%).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and low interest rates indicates strong demand fundamentals, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show negative correlation with GDPC1/FEDFUNDS co-movements (Rank IC < -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS > 130k) signals labor market strength, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS > 130k periods are negative (return < -0.3%).
